examples.title: Zero-allocation params with ReadOnlySpan<T>
examples.description: Switching from `params string[]` to `params ReadOnlySpan<string>` keeps call-site arguments on the stack and eliminates the hidden heap allocation on every invocation.

examples.title: Flexible aggregation with params IEnumerable<T>
examples.description: A `params IEnumerable<int>` helper accepts arrays, lists, and collection expressions at the call site without requiring callers to convert their data first.

relatedFeatures.reason: Start with Span and ReadOnlySpan to understand the stack-based types that make allocation-free params possible.
Continue to Collection expressions to see how C# 12 collection literals compose naturally with params collection parameters.
Explore Ref structs to understand the stack-only constraint that governs ReadOnlySpan<T> and Span<T> params parameters.
